medicines.name: Paracetamol
medicines.mechanismOfAction: Inhibits prostaglandin synthesis in CNS by blocking COX enzymes, reducing pain and fever

clinicalUseDetails.details: Acts centrally on hypothalamic heat-regulating center to reduce fever. Safe for all age groups including children and elderly. Minimal GI side effects make it ideal for OPD use.
Inhibits CNS prostaglandin synthesis without peripheral anti-inflammatory effects. Suitable for patients who cannot tolerate NSAIDs due to gastric issues or bleeding risk.

medicines.medicineAdvantage: Effective symptom control with minimal gastric side effects. Safe for children, pregnant women (Category B), and patients with peptic ulcer history. First-line choice for fever and mild pain in OPD settings.
medicines.teachingNotes: Chosen because it provides effective analgesia and antipyresis with minimal anti-inflammatory effects, making it safer for patients with gastric sensitivity compared to NSAIDs. However, avoid in liver disease due to hepatic metabolism.

medicines.name: Ibuprofen
medicines.mechanismOfAction: Non-selective COX-1 and COX-2 inhibitor that reduces prostaglandin synthesis, providing anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antipyretic effects

sexSpecificRisks: Increased risk of bleeding in females

clinicalUseDetails.details: Dual COX-1 and COX-2 inhibition provides potent anti-inflammatory action. Reduces prostaglandin-mediated inflammation at injury site. Best taken with food to minimize gastric irritation.

Effective for both osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis symptom management. Provides better pain relief than paracetamol for inflammatory joint conditions. Monitor for GI bleeding with long-term use.

medicines.medicineAdvantage: Superior anti-inflammatory action compared to paracetamol. Ideal for musculoskeletal injuries, arthritis, and dysmenorrhea. Short-term use safe for most adults. Avoid in pregnancy, peptic ulcer disease, and renal impairment.
medicines.teachingNotes: Selected for its anti-inflammatory properties which are superior to paracetamol for inflammatory conditions. However, avoid in pregnancy (especially third trimester) and in patients with gastric issues due to COX-1 inhibition causing gastric mucosal damage.

medicines.name: Amoxicillin
medicines.mechanismOfAction: Bactericidal antibiotic that inhibits bacterial cell wall synthesis by binding to penicillin-binding proteins
medicines.teachingNotes: First-line for many bacterial infections due to broad spectrum activity against gram-positive and some gram-negative bacteria. Avoid in penicillin allergy. Should not be used for viral infections as this contributes to antibiotic resistance.
